title: Chapter 8 Master Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of string methods and formatting.

questions.question: What does "hello".upper() return?
questions.explanation: .upper() converts all characters to uppercase.

questions.question: String methods modify the original string.
questions.explanation: Strings are immutable. Methods return a new string.

questions.question: What does "  hello  ".strip() return?
questions.explanation: strip() removes leading AND trailing whitespace.

questions.question: What does find() return if not found?
questions.explanation: find() returns -1 when the substring is not found.

questions.question: What does "hello".count("l") return?
questions.explanation: count() finds 2 occurrences of 'l' in 'hello'.

questions.question: What does "a-b-c".split("-") return?
questions.explanation: split('-') breaks the string at each '-' into a list.

questions.question: What does ",".join(["a","b"]) return?
questions.explanation: join() inserts the separator between elements: a,b.

questions.question: f"{3.14159:.2f}" formats the number to _____ decimal places.
questions.explanation: :.2f means format as float with 2 decimal places.

questions.question: How do you reverse a string?
questions.explanation: [::-1] slicing reverses any sequence including strings.

questions.question: "123".isdigit() returns True.
questions.explanation: .isdigit() returns True when all characters are digits.

questions.question: What does .replace() do?
options: Replaces all occurrences of a substring
questions.explanation: .replace(old, new) replaces ALL occurrences of old with new.

questions.question: What does f"{1000000:,}" produce?
questions.explanation: The :, format specifier adds thousand separators.

questions.question: Which checks if a string starts with 'http'?
questions.explanation: .startswith() checks if a string begins with the given prefix.

questions.question: To split by default whitespace, use string._____().
questions.explanation: split() without arguments splits on whitespace.

questions.question: What does "Hello World".title() return?
questions.explanation: .title() capitalizes the first letter of each word.

questions.question: The .format() method is an older alternative to f-strings.
questions.explanation: .format() was the standard before f-strings (Python 3.6+).

questions.question: What does "abc".isalpha() return?
questions.explanation: .isalpha() returns True when all characters are letters.

questions.question: What is the difference between find() and index()?
options: find returns -1, index raises error if not found
questions.explanation: find() returns -1 when not found; index() raises a ValueError.

questions.question: What does " ".isspace() return?
questions.explanation: .isspace() returns True when all characters are whitespace.

questions.question: How do you check if 'word' is a palindrome?
questions.explanation: Compare the word with its reverse: word == word[::-1].

questions.question: To remove leading whitespace only, use ._____()
questions.explanation: .lstrip() removes leading (left) whitespace only.

questions.question: You can chain string methods like text.lower().strip().
questions.explanation: Since each method returns a new string, you can chain them together.
